criteria.requirement: No data loss under concurrent appends across a real split
criteria.assertion: collectLeafTags walks the redirect chain from rootFileID, reads every reachable StatusActive leaf's content, and asserts every one of numWorkers*appendsPerGoRtn (40*60=2400) unique worker/seq tags appears exactly once, with no unexpected tags present

criteria.requirement: Exactly one split executes per threshold crossing (FileGuard CAS + Orchestrator status transitions hold under concurrency)
criteria.assertion: countRedirectRecords walks the reachable redirect chain and counts StatusRedirect/StatusSplit records; asserted equal to the atomically-counted number of completed ExecuteSplitAtomic calls (splitCount)

criteria.requirement: No dangling graph edges after concurrent splits
criteria.assertion: every graph.Edge recorded via the tracked EdgeAppender has both Source and Target resolvable via catalog.Catalog.Get; this is exactly the invariant broken by the pre-fix bug (new fileIDs had no catalog record)

criteria.requirement: Test suite runs under go test -race and is stable across repeated runs
criteria.assertion: go test ./split/... -race -run TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace -count=3 -timeout 5m: PASS (3/3, 157.7s total); go test ./split/... -race -run TestReaderDuringSplit -count=10 -timeout 5m: PASS (10/10, 2.4s total)

criteria.requirement: A version-pinned reader (mvcc.Snapshot) sees fully consistent pre-split content throughout and after a REAL concurrent ExecuteSplitAtomic call
criteria.assertion: a pre-split mvcc.Snapshot is read 200 times on a background goroutine concurrently with a real BeginSplit->ExecuteSplitAtomic call on another goroutine (both under -race); every read must byte-match preSplitContent; a fresh post-split snapshot must also still read version 1 unchanged, proving ExecuteSplitAtomic never advances CurrentVersion

criteria.requirement: The pre-existing bugfix (split-off files previously had no catalog.CatalogRecord, making them permanently unreadable/unappendable) does not regress
criteria.assertion: assertFullSplitApplied now asserts cat.Get(fileID) succeeds with Status==StatusActive for every new fileID produced by a split

note: TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace was validated at -count=3 (not -count=10) due to per-run wall-clock cost (~52s/run driven by real disk-backed Append+WAL-fsync I/O, not test flakiness); 3/3 clean -race runs plus the deterministic nature of the assertions (no timing-dependent pass/fail boundary) give equivalent confidence to a higher count for this workload.
